questions.question_text: Shifting tectonic plates 'trigger' earthquakes that can destroy entire communities. What does 'trigger' mean?

options.text: To prevent and stop an event from happening naturally
To cause something to start or happen suddenly
To slow down and reduce the impact of an event
To predict and prepare for something in advance

questions.explanation_ar: trigger تعني يُفجّر أو يُحرّك، أي أن يتسبب في بدء حدث ما أو إطلاقه بشكل مفاجئ.

questions.question_text: Major earthquakes can 'devastate' entire communities within seconds. What does 'devastate' mean?

options.text: To evacuate people safely before an event occurs
To warn people about the risk of a natural disaster
To cause severe destruction and widespread damage to a place
To rebuild and repair damage caused by a natural event

questions.explanation_ar: devastate تعني يُدمّر ويُحدث خراباً واسعاً، أي إلحاق ضرر شديد وواسع النطاق بمنطقة أو مجتمع بأكمله.

questions.question_text: The 'magnitude' of an earthquake is measured on the Richter scale. What does 'magnitude' mean?

options.text: The duration of time that shaking lasts during an earthquake
The distance from the epicentre to the nearest populated area
The depth at which the earthquake originates underground
The size or strength of an earthquake and the energy it releases

questions.explanation_ar: magnitude تعني الحجم أو الشدة، وفي سياق الزلازل تُشير إلى كمية الطاقة المُطلَقة التي تحدد قوة الزلزال.

questions.question_text: Seismologists study the 'phenomenon' of earthquake waves travelling through the Earth. What does 'phenomenon' mean?

options.text: A fact or event that can be observed and studied scientifically
A tool used by scientists to measure ground movement
A scientific theory that has not yet been proven
A physical location where earthquakes occur most frequently

questions.explanation_ar: phenomenon تعني ظاهرة، أي حدث أو واقعة يمكن ملاحظتها ودراستها وتحليلها من منظور علمي.

questions.question_text: Ground movement levels can 'fluctuate' during seismic events. What does 'fluctuate' mean?

options.text: To remain completely stable and unchanged over time
To rise and fall irregularly without following a fixed pattern
To increase steadily and continuously without stopping
To decrease slowly until it reaches zero permanently

questions.explanation_ar: fluctuate تعني يتذبذب ويتفاوت، أي أن يتغير بشكل غير منتظم صعوداً وهبوطاً دون اتباع نمط ثابت.

questions.question_text: When tectonic plates 'converge', they are moving toward each other and colliding in the same location.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. converge تعني يتقاطع أو يتلاقى، أي أن الأشياء تتحرك نحو بعضها وتلتقي في نقطة واحدة.

questions.question_text: When scientists 'assess' earthquake risks, they are actively damaging buildings to test their strength.
questions.explanation_ar: خطأ. assess تعني يُقيّم ويدرس، أي تحليل الوضع وتقدير المخاطر باستخدام البيانات والملاحظات وليس إلحاق الضرر.

questions.question_text: Countries with poor building standards tend to _____ much worse during earthquakes than those with strict codes.
questions.explanation_ar: fare تعني يُحقّق نتيجة أو يُسجّل أداءً معيناً في موقف ما، وتُستخدم للتعبير عن مدى النجاح أو الفشل في ظروف صعبة.

questions.question_text: Which sentence correctly uses the second conditional?

options.text: If the earthquake happens, we will evacuate the building immediately.
If scientists discovered a reliable prediction method, they would save thousands of lives.
If the building collapses, people will be in serious danger.
If we prepare emergency kits, we can be safer during earthquakes.

questions.explanation_ar: الشرط الثاني يُستخدم للحالات الافتراضية وغير الواقعية باستخدام ماضٍ بسيط في جملة الشرط و would + مصدر في الجواب. الخيار b هو الصحيح، بينما الأخرى تستخدم الشرط الأول.

questions.question_text: Complete: 'If the building _____ more flexible, it _____ the earthquake better.'

options.text: is / will withstand
was / withstood
were / would withstand
will be / would withstand

questions.explanation_ar: في الشرط الثاني نستخدم were لفعل الكينونة في جملة الشرط (بدلاً من was في الإنجليزية الرسمية)، ويليها would + مصدر في الجواب.

questions.question_text: Which sentence expresses advice using the second conditional correctly?

options.text: If I will be an engineer, I would design earthquake-proof buildings.
If I am an engineer, I would design earthquake-proof buildings.
If I were an engineer, I would design earthquake-proof buildings.
If I were an engineer, I will design earthquake-proof buildings.

questions.explanation_ar: في الشرط الثاني نستخدم were في جملة الشرط (بدلاً من was) مع أي ضمير، ويأتي would + مصدر في الجواب دون will.

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses the FIRST conditional, not the second?

options.text: If seismologists had better tools, they would predict earthquakes precisely.
If the ground moved less, buildings would suffer far less damage.
If there were no tectonic plates, earthquakes would not exist at all.
If the warning alarm sounds, residents will move to safe zones immediately.

questions.explanation_ar: الشرط الأول يستخدم المضارع البسيط في جملة الشرط و will + مصدر في الجواب، وهو للمواقف المحتملة الحدوث. الخيار d هو الشرط الأول، بينما الأخرى تستخدم الشرط الثاني.

questions.question_text: 'If scientists were able to predict earthquakes accurately, thousands of lives would be saved every year' uses the second conditional correctly.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. were able في جملة الشرط و would be saved في الجواب يُشكّلان الشرط الثاني الصحيح للتعبير عن موقف افتراضي غير واقعي.

questions.question_text: Match each second conditional if-clause to the result clause that correctly completes it.

left_items: If all buildings had base isolation systems,
right_items: there would be no earthquakes on Earth.
more buildings would collapse and cause casualties.
earthquake damage would be significantly reduced.
I would dedicate my life to predicting seismic events.

correct_answer.left: If all buildings had base isolation systems,
correct_answer.right: earthquake damage would be significantly reduced.
there would be no earthquakes on Earth.
I would dedicate my life to predicting seismic events.
more buildings would collapse and cause casualties.

questions.explanation_ar: في الشرط الثاني تترتب على كل جملة شرط نتيجة منطقية افتراضية تُعبّر عنها بـ would + مصدر.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, where is the majority of earthquake activity concentrated globally?

options.text: Along the coast of the Arabian Peninsula
Across the continent of Africa
Around the Pacific Ocean, in the Ring of Fire
Along the borders between Europe and Asia

questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن معظم الزلازل تحدث على طول ما يُسمى 'حلقة النار' المحيطة بالمحيط الهادئ، حيث تتقاطع الصفائح التكتونية.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, why does the Arabian Peninsula experience occasional earthquakes?

options.text: It is located in the centre of the Pacific Ring of Fire
It sits on its own tectonic plate, which shifts independently
It has a weak rock structure that is easily disturbed by heat
It borders the Ring of Fire directly on its eastern side

questions.explanation_ar: أوضح النص أن شبه الجزيرة العربية تقع على صفيحة تكتونية خاصة بها، مما يُفسّر تعرّضها لأحداث زلزالية من حين لآخر.

questions.question_text: Passage A says that predicting when earthquakes will occur 'remains one of science's greatest challenges.' What does this suggest?

options.text: Scientists have recently developed a reliable earthquake prediction system
Earthquake prediction is now considered an unnecessary scientific goal
Despite advances, science cannot yet reliably forecast earthquake timing
Earthquakes can now be prevented using modern technology

questions.explanation_ar: تُضمّن هذه العبارة أنه رغم التطورات الهائلة في علم الزلازل، لا يزال التنبؤ الدقيق بموعد وقوع الزلازل خارج متناول العلم حتى الآن.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, the most devastating earthquake in recorded history occurred in China in 1556, killing approximately 830,000 people.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. ذكر النص أن أشد الزلازل تدميراً في التاريخ المسجَّل وقع في الصين عام 1556 وأودى بحياة ما يقارب 830,000 شخص.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, the Tokyo Skytree is a _____ -meter tower that sways safely during earthquakes.
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن برج طوكيو سكاي تري يبلغ ارتفاعه 634 متراً ويتأرجح بأمان أثناء الزلازل بدلاً من مقاومة الاهتزازات.

questions.question_text: Match each earthquake wave type or term from Passage A to its correct description.

right_items: instrument used to detect and record ground movement
measure of earthquake size based on wave amplitude
waves that arrive first and travel fastest through Earth
waves that follow P-waves and typically cause more damage

correct_answer.right: waves that arrive first and travel fastest through Earth
waves that follow P-waves and typically cause more damage
measure of earthquake size based on wave amplitude
instrument used to detect and record ground movement

questions.explanation_ar: وردت هذه المصطلحات في النص مع تعريفاتها: الموجات P الأسرع وصولاً، الموجات S الأشد تدميراً، مقياس ريختر لقياس الحجم، والسيزموميتر لرصد الاهتزازات.
